feed.title: Pronounce — recent dictionary additions
feed.subtitle: How engineers actually pronounce project / product / jargon names. Community-maintained.

entry.title: riemann — ree mahn
entry.summary: riemann is pronounced "ree mahn" (/ˈriːmɑːn/). Mathematician Riemann; "REE-mahn". German.

entry.title: ablation — uh blay shun
entry.summary: ablation is pronounced "uh blay shun" (/əˈbleɪʃən/). Ablation study: remove a part to measure its effect; "uh-BLAY-shun".

entry.title: annealing — uh nee ling
entry.summary: annealing is pronounced "uh nee ling" (/əˈniːlɪŋ/). Simulated annealing (optimization); "uh-NEE-ling".

entry.title: asymptotic — ass im tot ik
entry.summary: asymptotic is pronounced "ass im tot ik" (/ˌæsɪmpˈtɒtɪk/). Behavior in the limit; "ass-im-TOT-ik".

entry.title: backprop — back prop
entry.summary: backprop is pronounced "back prop" (/ˈbækprɒp/). Short for backpropagation; "back-prop".

entry.title: backpropagation — back prop uh gay shun
entry.summary: backpropagation is pronounced "back prop uh gay shun" (/ˌbækprɒpəˈɡeɪʃən/). Gradient computation through the network; "back-prop-uh-GAY-shun".

entry.title: centroid — sen troyd
entry.summary: centroid is pronounced "sen troyd" (/ˈsɛntrɔɪd/). Cluster center (k-means); "SEN-troyd".

entry.title: corpus — kor pus
entry.summary: corpus is pronounced "kor pus" (/ˈkɔːrpəs/). A body of text/data; "KOR-pus". Plural: corpora.

entry.title: corpora — kor per uh
entry.summary: corpora is pronounced "kor per uh" (/ˈkɔːrpərə/). Plural of corpus; "KOR-per-uh".

entry.title: cosine — koh sine
entry.summary: cosine is pronounced "koh sine" (/ˈkoʊsaɪn/). Cosine similarity; "KOH-sine".

entry.title: covariance — koh vair ee uns
entry.summary: covariance is pronounced "koh vair ee uns" (/koʊˈvɛəriəns/). "koh-VAIR-ee-uns".

entry.title: determinant — dih ter mih nunt
entry.summary: determinant is pronounced "dih ter mih nunt" (/dɪˈtɜːrmɪnənt/). Scalar from a square matrix; "dih-TER-mih-nunt".

entry.title: embedding — im bed ing
entry.summary: embedding is pronounced "im bed ing" (/ɪmˈbɛdɪŋ/). Dense vector representation; "im-BED-ing".

entry.title: ensemble — on som bul
entry.summary: ensemble is pronounced "on som bul" (/ɒnˈsɒmbəl/). Combine multiple models; "on-SOM-bul". French.

entry.title: entropy — en truh pee
entry.summary: entropy is pronounced "en truh pee" (/ˈɛntrəpi/). Measure of uncertainty (cross-entropy loss); "EN-truh-pee".

entry.title: hessian — hesh un
entry.summary: hessian is pronounced "hesh un" (/ˈhɛʃən/). Matrix of second-order partial derivatives; "HESH-un".

entry.title: hyperparameter — hyper puh ram ih ter
entry.summary: hyperparameter is pronounced "hyper puh ram ih ter" (/ˈhaɪpərpəˌræmɪtər/). A setting chosen before training (lr, batch size); "hyper-puh-RAM-ih-ter".

entry.title: inference — in fer uns
entry.summary: inference is pronounced "in fer uns" (/ˈɪnfərəns/). Running a trained model on new data; "IN-fer-uns".

entry.title: laplace — luh plahss
entry.summary: laplace is pronounced "luh plahss" (/ləˈplɑːs/). Mathematician Laplace; "luh-PLAHSS". French.

entry.title: laplacian — luh plah see un
entry.summary: laplacian is pronounced "luh plah see un" (/ləˈplɑːsiən/). "luh-PLAH-see-un" (graph Laplacian).

entry.title: latent — lay tunt
entry.summary: latent is pronounced "lay tunt" (/ˈleɪtənt/). Hidden (latent space, latent diffusion); "LAY-tunt".

entry.title: manifold — man ih fold
entry.summary: manifold is pronounced "man ih fold" (/ˈmænɪfoʊld/). A curved space (manifold hypothesis); "MAN-ih-fold".

entry.title: scalar — skay ler
entry.summary: scalar is pronounced "skay ler" (/ˈskeɪlər/). A single number; "SKAY-ler".

entry.title: sigmoid — sig moyd
entry.summary: sigmoid is pronounced "sig moyd" (/ˈsɪɡmɔɪd/). S-shaped activation function; "SIG-moyd".

entry.title: tokenization — toh kun eye zay shun
entry.summary: tokenization is pronounced "toh kun eye zay shun" (/ˌtoʊkənaɪˈzeɪʃən/). Splitting text into tokens; "toh-kun-eye-ZAY-shun".
